title: HTML Doc Components Sample
subtitle: A gallery document showing every supported component in the html-doc-coauthoring skill.
audience: Anyone who wants to see what visual documentation blocks Agent Kit can generate.
summary: This sample demonstrates the document shell plus every supported block type: markdown, cards, metrics, table, callout, image, cloud, flow, timeline, chart, steps, code, checklist, resources, and details.

sections.title: Document Shell
sections.intro: Top-level metadata controls the title, subtitle, audience note, badges, summary, navigation, and hero actions.

items.label: Chart variants
items.hint: Bar, line, pie, doughnut, radar, polar area

items.title: Title and subtitle
items.body: Shown in the sidebar and main hero area.

items.title: Audience and summary
items.body: Helps a new reader understand whether the document is for them.

items.title: Badges and actions
items.body: Badges summarize document type. Actions link to important sections or next steps.

rows: Reader profile shown in the sidebar

sections.title: Component Index
sections.intro: A quick inventory of every block supported by the generator and the variants worth testing.

rows: Prose, lists, quotes, and inline formatting
Headings, bold, italic, links, inline code, code fences, bullets, numbers, quotes, string shorthand
Headers, rows, and markdown inside cells
Groups, nested groups, node kinds, edge labels, layout, source disclosure
Checked, unchecked, object items, and string shorthand

blocks.title: Aliases
blocks.content: The cloud renderer also accepts `cloud-diagram`, `infrastructure`, and `infra`. The details renderer also accepts `accordion`. This sample uses canonical names plus an accordion alias example.

sections.title: Markdown
sections.intro: Use markdown blocks for concise prose, bullets, numbered lists, headings, inline formatting, code fences, and quotes.

blocks.content:
## Markdown block

This paragraph shows **bold text**, *italic text*, inline `code`, and an inline link to [Charts](#charts). Keep paragraphs short so the generated HTML stays scannable.

### Lists

- Bullet items are supported
- Inline links can point to anchors
- Use lists when readers need quick scanning

1. First numbered step
2. Second numbered step
3. Third numbered step

### Code fence inside markdown

```bash
python3 custom/skills/html-doc-coauthoring/scripts/build_html_doc.py docs/raw/sample-components-documentation.json
```

> Blockquotes work well for short notes or memorable guidance.

blocks: String blocks render as markdown too. Use them for small prose-only notes when an explicit `{"type": "markdown"}` wrapper would be noisy.

sections.title: Card Layouts
sections.intro: Cards can render in one, two, three, or four columns depending on the `columns` value.

items.title: One-column card
items.body: Use one column when a single idea needs room for a longer explanation.

items.title: Two-column card
items.body: Good for trade-offs, choices, owner pairs, or before-and-after summaries.

items.title: Second card
items.body: Cards support markdown bodies like **bold emphasis** and inline `code`.

items.title: Cards
items.body: Use cards for features, benefits, risks, options, responsibilities, or takeaways.

items.title: Metrics
items.body: Use metrics for memorable numeric facts such as counts, timelines, budgets, or KPIs.

items.title: Tables
items.body: Use tables when readers need exact comparisons or compact reference data.

items.title: Info
items.body: Neutral context.

items.title: Success
items.body: Approved or completed path.

items.title: Warning
items.body: Important constraints.

items.title: Danger
items.body: High-risk issues.

sections.title: Data Blocks
sections.intro: Metrics and tables turn structured facts into quick visual summaries.

rows: The reader needs exact tabular comparison
The value is not meaningful by itself
Comparisons, matrices, and compact reference data
Critical notes, decisions, warnings, and constraints

sections.title: Callouts
sections.intro: Callouts support four tones for different kinds of emphasis.

blocks.title: Info
blocks.content: Use `info` for neutral context, helpful notes, or reader orientation.

blocks.title: Success
blocks.content: Use `success` for approved decisions, completed milestones, or recommended paths.

blocks.title: Warning
blocks.content: Use `warning` for constraints, trade-offs, or things a reader should verify before acting.

blocks.title: Danger
blocks.content: Use `danger` for high-risk issues such as security concerns, data loss, or irreversible actions.

sections.title: Process Blocks
sections.intro: Flow, timeline, and steps blocks explain motion through time or sequence.

items.title: Collect context
items.body: Gather audience, goal, source material, and constraints.

items.title: Draft structure
items.body: Choose sections and visual blocks that fit the reader journey.

items.title: Generate HTML
items.body: Save compact JSON under `docs/raw/` and run the generator.

items.title: Review
items.body: Open the generated HTML and check that the document is clear.

items.title: Outline
items.body: Agree on the document purpose and audience.

items.title: Draft
items.body: Write concise section content and choose visuals.

items.title: Publish
items.body: Generate HTML and share the output path.

items.title: Write JSON
items.body: Create `docs/raw/<doc-name>.json` with title, summary, sections, and blocks.

items.title: Run generator
items.body: Run `python3 custom/skills/html-doc-coauthoring/scripts/build_html_doc.py docs/raw/<doc-name>.json`.

items.title: Open HTML
items.body: Review `docs/<doc-name>.html` in a browser.

sections.title: Charts
sections.intro: The chart block uses Chart.js. This sample shows all supported chart variants from the skill guide.

sections.title: Cloud Topology
sections.intro: Cloud blocks render Cytoscape topology diagrams with groups, nested groups, node kinds, edge labels, layout direction, and optional source disclosure.

blocks.title: AWS production infrastructure
blocks.caption: This single diagram demonstrates every built-in cloud node kind while keeping a realistic AWS request and delivery path.

blocks.title: Production guardrail
blocks.content: Keep compute, databases, queues, and cache services in private subnets. Only edge and load-balancing services should be internet-facing.

sections.title: Images
sections.intro: Image blocks are useful for screenshots, diagrams exported from other tools, and local visual assets.

blocks.title: Local image block
blocks.caption: Images support safe local or web paths, alt text, an optional title, and a caption.

sections.title: Review and Reference Blocks
sections.intro: Checklist, resources, and details blocks support review workflows, related links, and optional depth.

items.label: All supported block types are represented
items.body: Includes the explicit `markdown` block and string shorthand.

items.label: Cloud nodes use `type` or `kind` for styling
items.body: The topology legend now shows meaningful node kinds instead of generic services.

items.label: Generated HTML reviewed in a browser
items.body: Open the generated page and inspect the interactive chart and cloud blocks.

items.label: HTML doc guide
items.description: Reference for the JSON shape and block guidance.

items.label: Generator script
items.description: Renderer implementation for every supported component.

items.label: Charts section
items.description: Jump to all Chart.js examples in this sample.

items.label: Cloud topology section
items.description: Jump to the Cytoscape cloud diagram example.

items.title: When should I use details?
items.body: Use details for optional background, caveats, implementation notes, or long explanations that should not distract from the main path.

items.title: Can details contain markdown?
items.body: Yes. Details bodies support **bold**, *italic*, inline `code`, lists, and links like [Component index](#component-index).

items.title: Alias support
items.body: The generator treats `accordion` as an alias for `details`, so both render as expandable disclosure panels.

sections.title: Code
sections.intro: Code blocks are for exact commands, config, API examples, or snippets that readers may copy.

blocks.content: Internet -> Edge -> Load balancer -> Private app -> Data stores -> Observability
